item.title: Licensing Junior Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded: The image in this article has been sourced to https://www.flickr.com/photos/xolta/6109873646/in/pool-951756@N22 and is not compliant with our license. It has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It can also be 紅炎, Red Flame, which is never first things on your mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This actually gives a really good life advice… Never watch bootleg Godzilla.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting idea. Reminds me of Wittgenstein's "Beetle in a Box" thought experiment.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1154 is the designation given to a collection of media which includes poems, short stories, a novel and a VHS tape.

SCP-1154's anomalous effects become apparent when it is viewed and understood by a human being, who will hereafter be referred to as the subject.

This sounds like you have to be exposed to the entire collection of works to trigger the effect. If that's true, then the note about still images not carrying the effect isn't necessary- the article could cite any number of SCP-1154 works, in their entirety, and still not spread the effect as long as one or more works was left out.

If that's not true, and the effect can manifest after exposure to an individual work or a subset of the collection, then the second quoted sentence needs rephrasing to make that clear. I would write "SCP-1154's anomalous effects become apparent when an SCP-1154 instance is viewed and understood," etc., if a single work is sufficient to infect a subject, or specify a number of understood pieces or other threshold for what level of exposure triggers the effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. Interesting. So it's a bunch of stories that make you get eaten by a nonexistent dragon? I like how the stories all connect by everybody being eaten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you not familiar with draconic mythology? They have a reputation for stealing valuable objects. Effectively, this is an article about a spiritual dragon that stalks people who get its attention by interacting with a specific set of objects. Kind of like The Ring, except replace the scrawny ghost chick with a dragon phantom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it. I can't even tell what is even happening here. It just feels like an assortment of arbitrary things put together for mysterious reasons that aren't even hinted at in the article. I'm pretty sure the idea of "scary thing wot haunts/changes you afterwards" has been done and done more coherently. This one reads more like a brainstorming session arranged coherently.

Example: the randomly missing jewelry. This actually gave me an SCP-type idea, yet here it's completely extraneous and there for no reason. It could be replaced with "Projectile vomiting and cephalic-methane emissions" and absolutely nothing else in the article would be affected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty underrated SCP, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I keep forgetting how good this one is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm only sorry that I didn't find this one sooner. I'm a sucker for a well done Dragon skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if it's just my natural affinity with dragons, but I really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It hits my favorite theme of forbidden knowledge. You know about the dragon, and now it knows about you.

It stalks you, it takes your treasures, and you will feel its hot breath on your neck until it finally gets you.

The only way it could get better if there was a survivor who figured out how to keep the dragon away, but at a terrible price. Like a shifty, nervous man who keeps kidnapping young maidens off the street.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They are probably related, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, sorry. It really pull me in, but it's not 'bad'. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing that makes me angry in this SCP is that you beat me to an interesting dragon-themed SCP, you bastard.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perceiving a drastic increase in the temperature

Increase in the temperature of what? His own or the ambient temperature?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. Good thread through the components, and I particularly like the kaiju angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted

I have two suggestions though: the traditional image of a dragon in the european/christian culture is diametrically opposed to the traditional image of the dragon in the chinese culture. I think you're reffering to the first view. Also unexplained scratch or bite marks are to be expected on the subjects bodies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The name 'Conceptual Dragon' reminds me of the Raw Shark Texts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, minor language quibble, when I read "kouen" in the context of something about Japan, that's the standard romaji for 公園, which means "public park". Were you going for something particular there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds more like SCP-1128's third cousin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brother-in-law, I'm guessing. There isn't much family resemblance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 682's brother?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something scary about being stalked and eaten by an invisible dragon. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this idea apart from the picture, I'd go with a drawing of a dragon with odd features and the Godzilla film reference.

Upvoted.
